codeReview.summary: Audited channel.rs and handle.rs with one real bug found and fixed (closed_count saturation) and 21 new tests. The fix is correct and minimal — an early-return guard in close_endpoint when closed_count >= 2 prevents page double-free on redundant calls. handle.rs confirmed clean (0 unsafe, pure safe Rust). Tests cover all areas specified in the feature description: close-while-waiting race, signal on half-closed channel, closed_count saturation, encoding edge cases, multiple signal coalescing, waiter lifecycle, use-after-close, slot reuse, max slot, rights checking, full drain-refill lifecycle. Cross-module analysis (handle close during channel operation) verified sound via lock ordering argument.

issues.description: close_wakes_peer_waiter test manually replicates close_endpoint logic (clearing own waiter, taking peer waiter) instead of calling the model's close_endpoint method. If close_endpoint's implementation changed, this test could still pass. Mitigated by close_takes_peer_waiter_for_waking which does call close_endpoint correctly.

register_after_close_is_no_op: test documents that registering a waiter on a closed endpoint stores the ThreadId (test asserts Some(ThreadId(42))). In the kernel, this waiter will never be consumed/woken. While harmless in practice (requires deliberate misuse), it's a latent resource leak pattern worth noting for future design review.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Handoff text says '10 channel + 11 handle' tests but actual counts are 11 channel + 10 handle (total 21 correct). Minor handoff documentation imprecision — no code impact.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff salientSummary: '21 new tests added' and whatWasImplemented: '21 new tests (10 channel + 11 handle)' vs diff showing 11 channel test functions and 10 handle test functions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Test close_wakes_peer_waiter manually manipulates struct fields instead of calling the model's close_endpoint method. AGENTS.md notes 'follow the structure of existing test files' but doesn't explicitly state that model methods should be preferred over field-level manipulation when the model provides a suitable method.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/test/tests/channel.rs:330-345 — test manually does ch.waiter[ep] = None and ch.waiter[peer_ep].take() instead of calling ch.close_endpoint(ep0).

summary: Pass. One real bug found and correctly fixed: closed_count saturation guard in close_endpoint prevents page double-free on redundant close calls. Fix is minimal (6-line early-return guard) and covered by a dedicated test that verifies TDD (failed before fix, passes after). 21 new tests thoroughly cover all feature description requirements: shared memory page safety, close-while-blocked-reader races, capacity/overflow, handle lifecycle, handle table growth, concurrent access safety, and cross-module interaction. Two non-blocking observations: one test manually replicates logic instead of using the model method, and registering a waiter on a closed endpoint silently stores a dead reference. Worker followed skill procedure correctly.
